file_insights.primary_purpose: Core authentication service handling JWT generation, validation, password hashing, session management, and authentication flows
file_insights.architectural_role: Core service in dependency injection container

patterns_discovered.description: @injectable() and @inject() decorators for IoC container management
patterns_discovered.benefit: Loose coupling, testability, centralized dependency management

patterns_discovered.description: Session and token data stored in Redis with TTL expiration using key prefixes
patterns_discovered.benefit: Scalable distributed session handling, automatic cleanup via TTL

patterns_discovered.description: Using unique token IDs (jti claims) with 'tok_' prefix for tracking revocation
patterns_discovered.benefit: Fine-grained token revocation control, prevents token reuse

patterns_discovered.description: Accepting getUserData callback in refreshAccessToken instead of direct injection
patterns_discovered.benefit: Flexible user data loading, decouples from user service dependency

patterns_discovered.description: Simultaneous generation of short-lived access tokens and long-lived refresh tokens
patterns_discovered.benefit: Standard OAuth pattern, better security with token rotation

patterns_discovered.location: Throughout entire file, every public method
patterns_discovered.benefit: Developer experience, IDE autocomplete support, clear API contracts

patterns_discovered.description: Redis key prefixes and TTL constants centralized in Constants section
patterns_discovered.benefit: Maintainability, prevents magic strings, single source of truth

patterns_discovered.description: Code divided into logical sections with clear comments (password management, token generation, etc.)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Readability, navigation, clear separation of concerns

gotchas_discovered.description: Refresh tokens are revoked immediately after use. Concurrent refresh requests could fail if not handled correctly
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Client should not make concurrent refresh requests; implement debouncing on client side

gotchas_discovered.description: Blacklist TTL calculated as (exp - now) which requires system time consistency and proper JWT exp claim
gotchas_discovered.risk: If system clock skews, tokens could expire before being properly blacklisted

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: User data can change between token generation and refresh
gotchas_discovered.description: refreshAccessToken fetches current user data, meaning roles/permissions could change mid-session
gotchas_discovered.consideration: This is intentional design (fresh data), but should be documented to users

gotchas_discovered.description: revokeAllUserTokens uses pattern scanning which could be slow with millions of keys
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Consider using Redis streams or maintaining user:token reverse index for large-scale deployments

gotchas_discovered.description: Reset and verification tokens are simple crypto tokens stored in Redis - ensure Redis is properly secured
gotchas_discovered.risk: Redis compromise exposes sensitive tokens; consider additional encryption

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: No rate limiting on token generation
gotchas_discovered.description: generateTokenPair can be called unlimited times without throttling
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Implement rate limiting at controller/middleware level

approach_outcome.completion_notes: Successfully created comprehensive AuthService with full JWT lifecycle management
key_accomplishments: Created JWT token generation for access and refresh tokens
Built session management layer using Redis
Implemented token revocation with blacklist support
Created password reset and email verification token generation
Implemented force logout across all user sessions
Complete JSDoc documentation with usage examples

code_quality_metrics.documentation_coverage: Excellent - every public method has JSDoc with examples
code_quality_metrics.type_safety: Strong - comprehensive TypeScript interfaces and types
code_quality_metrics.error_handling: Good - uses custom AppException for specific error codes
code_quality_metrics.testing_readiness: High - clear method contracts, dependency injection ready

recommendations.recommendation: Implement rate limiting for token generation endpoints at the controller/middleware level
recommendations.rationale: Prevents brute force attacks and token generation abuse

recommendations.recommendation: For large deployments, replace Redis pattern scanning with a user:tokens reverse index
recommendations.rationale: revokeAllUserTokens uses pattern scanning which is O(n) - reverse index would be O(1)

recommendations.recommendation: Consider encrypting sensitive tokens (password reset, email verification) in Redis
recommendations.rationale: Additional layer of security if Redis is compromised

recommendations.recommendation: Document the intentional behavior of refreshAccessToken fetching fresh user data
recommendations.rationale: Users should understand roles/permissions can change during session

recommendations.recommendation: Add client-side debouncing for refresh token requests to prevent race conditions
recommendations.rationale: Single-use tokens can fail with concurrent refresh attempts

recommendations.recommendation: Create comprehensive unit tests covering token expiration edge cases and race conditions
recommendations.rationale: Authentication is security-critical; edge cases should be thoroughly tested

recommendations.recommendation: Add logging/metrics for blacklist hit rates and session creation/destruction
recommendations.rationale: Helps detect unusual authentication patterns and potential attacks
